description_long: This object consists of two potted plants placed side by side. The larger plant features tall, upright, sword-like leaves with green and light yellow variegation, characteristic of a snake plant (Sansevieria trifasciata). The smaller plant is a round, green cactus with pronounced vertical ridges and short, yellowish spines, resembling a golden barrel cactus (Echinocactus grusonii). Both plants are housed in matching medium-brown terracotta pots with a simple, unadorned design and a shallow saucer base. The pots are filled with a visible layer of small, light-colored pebbles or gravel on the surface. The overall appearance is clean and well-maintained, suitable for decorative use in urban environments. No plant identification labels or tags are visible on either pot.
description: A pair of potted plants: a tall, variegated snake plant and a round, spiny cactus, both in plain brown terracotta pots with pebble topdressing.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the tall snake plant and the round cactus side by side in brown pots.
description_view_1: View with the cactus directly in front of the snake plant, partially obscuring the latter.
description_view_2: Reverse side of view 0, with the cactus on the left and the snake plant on the right, both fully visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the snake plant, with the cactus mostly hidden behind it.
attribute_plant_species: snake plant (Sansevieria trifasciata), golden barrel cactus (Echinocactus grusonii)
